connectorUiConfig.title: UniFi Syslog (CCF Push) (Preview)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The [UniFi Syslog](https://help.ui.com/hc/en-us/articles/33349041044119-UniFi-System-Logs-SIEM-Integration) connector for Microsoft Sentinel ingests UniFi UDM, switch and access-point syslog events using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) Push pattern.

UniFi devices forward syslog (UDP/TCP 514) to a Logstash relay you run on your own infrastructure (UnRAID, k8s, VM). Logstash parses the dual-timestamp UniFi format, normalises CEF and synthetic-CEF events, enriches with MaxMind GeoIP/ASN and FireHOL / Spamhaus / Tor threat-intel feeds at ingest time, and POSTs to the Data Collection Endpoint provisioned by this connector.

Security events land in **CommonSecurityLog** (CEF + synthetic CEF for sudo, dnsmasq-dhcp, AP kernel WiFi state, ubios gateway signals, switch link state, sshd, UniFi zone-based firewall). Forensic / non-CEF events land in **Unifi_Syslog_CL**.

sampleQueries.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor =~ 'Ubiquiti'
| where DeviceEventCategory =~ 'Firewall'
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 50

CommonSecurityLog
| where isnotempty(MaliciousIP)
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| summarize Hits = count() by SourceIP, ThreatSeverity, IndicatorThreatType, ThreatDescription
| order by Hits desc

CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceEventClassID == 'sshd_auth_fail'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| summarize Attempts = count() by SourceIP, SourceUserName
| order by Attempts desc

Unifi_Syslog_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 50

dataTypes.name: CommonSecurityLog (Ubiquiti)
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor =~ 'Ubiquiti'
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: Unifi_Syslog_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
Unifi_Syslog_CL
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

value:
union
  (CommonSecurityLog | where DeviceVendor =~ 'Ubiquiti'),
  (Unifi_Syslog_CL)
| summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(7d)

instructionSteps.title: 1. Deploy Sentinel-side resources
instructionSteps.description: Click Deploy below to create the Entra application, Data Collection Rule, custom Unifi_Syslog_CL table, and role assignments in your subscription. This step does not deploy anything on your UniFi side.

parameters.content:
#### Automated configuration
Deploying will:
- Create the `Unifi_Syslog_CL` custom log table.
- Create the Data Collection Rule with two transforms - CEF events route to `CommonSecurityLog`, non-CEF events route to `Unifi_Syslog_CL`.
- Register an Entra application with a client secret.
- Grant the application `Monitoring Metrics Publisher` on the DCR.

At the end you'll receive the five connection values that Logstash needs.

instructionSteps.title: 3. Deploy the Logstash relay
instructionSteps.description: On the Linux host that will receive syslog from your UniFi devices, deploy the Logstash container shipped alongside this Solution.

parameters.content:
#### Container deployment

The container files ship inside this Solution at `Solutions/UniFi Syslog (CCF)/Logstash/`. From the Azure-Sentinel repo on your relay host:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel
cd "Azure-Sentinel/Solutions/UniFi Syslog (CCF)/Logstash"
```

Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in the five values from step 2 plus your MaxMind credentials:

```bash
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env
SENTINEL_TENANT_ID=<Tenant ID>
SENTINEL_CLIENT_APP_ID=<Application ID>
SENTINEL_CLIENT_APP_SECRET=<Client Secret>
SENTINEL_DCE_URL=<DCE URI>
SENTINEL_DCR_IMMUTABLE_ID=<DCR Immutable ID>
SENTINEL_DCR_STREAM_NAME=Custom-Unifi_CL
MAXMIND_ACCOUNT_ID=<your MaxMind account ID>
MAXMIND_LICENSE_KEY=<your MaxMind license key>
```

Start the container:

```bash
docker compose build --no-cache
docker compose up -d
```

Verify the container is listening on UDP/TCP 514 and check the Logstash API on port 9600 for pipeline event counts.

instructionSteps.title: 4. Point your UniFi devices at the Logstash host
instructionSteps.description: Configure UniFi to forward syslog to the Logstash relay's IP and port. UniFi exposes this in two places.

parameters.content:
#### Site-wide syslog
In the UniFi Network application, go to **Settings -> Logging -> Remote Logging**. Enable **Remote Logging**, set **Server** to the Logstash host IP and **Port** to 514. Tick **Security Detections** to include Suricata IDS / IPS alerts.

#### Console / OS
For UniFi OS console events (sshd, sudo, ubios-udapi-server, switch TRAPMGR), enable **System Settings -> Console Settings -> Remote Logging** with the same host and port.

#### Verify
Within 30 seconds you should see Logstash event counts climbing in `docker logs unifi-syslog-logstash`, and rows appearing in `CommonSecurityLog | where DeviceVendor =~ 'Ubiquiti'` and `Unifi_Syslog_CL` within 1-2 minutes.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | where EventFormat == 'cef' | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnotnull(EventTime), todatetime(EventTime), now()) | extend AdditionalExtensions = tostring(cef) | project TimeGenerated, DeviceVendor, DeviceProduct, DeviceVersion, DeviceEventClassID, Activity, LogSeverity = tostring(LogSeverity), OriginalLogSeverity = case(toint(LogSeverity) >= 9, 'Very-High', toint(LogSeverity) >= 7, 'High', toint(LogSeverity) >= 4, 'Medium', isnotempty(LogSeverity), 'Low', ''), SourceIP, SourcePort = toint(SourcePort), SourceUserName, SourceMACAddress, SourceHostName, DestinationIP, DestinationPort = toint(DestinationPort), DestinationUserName, DestinationMACAddress, DeviceMacAddress, DeviceAddress, DeviceName, DeviceInboundInterface, DeviceOutboundInterface, ApplicationProtocol, DestinationServiceName, Protocol, DeviceAction, DeviceEventCategory, Message, ReceiptTime, EventOutcome = Outcome, DeviceCustomString1, DeviceCustomString2, DeviceCustomString3, DeviceCustomString4, DeviceCustomString5, DeviceCustomString6, DeviceCustomNumber1 = iff(isnotempty(DeviceCustomNumber1), toint(DeviceCustomNumber1), int(null)), MaliciousIP, ThreatSeverity = iff(isnotempty(ThreatSeverity), toint(ThreatSeverity), int(null)), IndicatorThreatType, ThreatDescription, ThreatConfidence, ReportReferenceLink, MaliciousIPCountry, MaliciousIPLatitude = iff(isnotempty(MaliciousIPLatitude), toreal(MaliciousIPLatitude), real(null)), MaliciousIPLongitude = iff(isnotempty(MaliciousIPLongitude), toreal(MaliciousIPLongitude), real(null)), AdditionalExtensions, Computer

source | where EventFormat != 'cef' or isnull(EventFormat) | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnotnull(EventTime), todatetime(EventTime), now()) | extend RawJson = rawJson | project TimeGenerated, EventTime, Computer, Facility, SyslogSeverity, ProcessName, SourceIP, SourcePort = iff(isnotempty(SourcePort), toint(SourcePort), int(null)), SourceUserName, DestinationIP, DestinationPort = iff(isnotempty(DestinationPort), toint(DestinationPort), int(null)), DestinationUserName, Protocol, DeviceAction, DeviceEventCategory, Message, RawJson

columns.name: EventTime
columns.description: Original event timestamp parsed from the syslog header

columns.name: Facility
columns.description: Syslog facility name (kern, daemon, auth, etc.)

columns.name: SourceIP
columns.description: Source IP extracted from the event payload

columns.name: RawJson
columns.description: Forensic JSON dump of fields not promoted to columns

instructionSteps.description: Configure UniFi to forward syslog to the Logstash relay's IP and port. UniFi exposes this in two places.

parameters.content:
#### Site-wide syslog
In the UniFi Network application, go to **Settings -> Logging -> Remote Logging**. Enable **Remote Logging**, set **Server** to the Logstash host IP and **Port** to 514. Tick **Security Detections** to include Suricata IDS / IPS alerts.

#### Console / OS
For UniFi OS console events (sshd, sudo, ubios-udapi-server, switch TRAPMGR), enable **System Settings -> Console Settings -> Remote Logging** with the same host and port.

#### Verify
Within 30 seconds you should see Logstash event counts climbing in `docker logs unifi-syslog-logstash`, and rows appearing in `CommonSecurityLog | where DeviceVendor =~ 'Ubiquiti'` and `Unifi_Syslog_CL` within 1-2 minutes.

properties.description: Identifies inbound traffic whose source IP matched a known-bad TI feed (FireHOL Level 1, Spamhaus DROP/EDROP, or Tor exit). Severity reflects the highest-confidence feed that matched. Tune by changing the ThreatSeverity threshold.
properties.displayName: UniFi: Threat-intel match on inbound traffic
properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where isnotempty(MaliciousIP) and ThreatSeverity >= 7
| summarize Hits = count(),
            FeedsMatched = make_set(ThreatDescription),
            TopActivity = take_any(Activity),
            TopAction   = take_any(DeviceAction),
            TargetPorts = make_set(DestinationPort, 25),
            TargetUsers = make_set(SourceUserName, 25)
  by SourceIP, MaliciousIPCountry, IndicatorThreatType,
     ASN_Org = DeviceCustomString4, ThreatSeverity
| order by ThreatSeverity desc, Hits desc

properties.displayName: UniFi: Threat-intel match on inbound traffic

properties.description: Identifies UniFi Network IDS (Suricata + CINS / Emerging Threats rulesets) detections. UniFi marks these severity 3 natively - this rule promotes them to Medium for incident handling. Also covers Suricata events that leak via plain syslog.
properties.query:
let cef =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where DeviceVendor =~ "Ubiquiti" and DeviceProduct =~ "UniFi Network"
    | where DeviceEventClassID == "201"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, SourceIP, SourcePort, DestinationIP, DestinationPort,
              Protocol, Signature = Activity, Category = DeviceEventCategory,
              Message, MaliciousIPCountry, IndicatorThreatType;
let suricata =
    Unifi_Syslog_CL
    | where ProcessName =~ "suricata" or isnotempty(RawJson.alert.signature)
    | extend Signature = tostring(RawJson.alert.signature),
             Category  = tostring(RawJson.alert.category)
    | where isnotempty(Signature)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, SourceIP, SourcePort, DestinationIP, DestinationPort,
              Protocol, Signature, Category, Message,
              MaliciousIPCountry = "", IndicatorThreatType = "";
union cef, suricata

properties.description: UnifiSyslogSshBruteForce_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0
Identifies 5+ SSH authentication failures from a single source IP within 15 minutes against UniFi consoles. Severity elevated to Critical when the source IP also matches a TI feed.
properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceEventClassID == "sshd_auth_fail"
| summarize Attempts = count(),
            TargetUsers = make_set(SourceUserName, 25),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            Country = take_any(coalesce(MaliciousIPCountry, DeviceCustomString3)),
            ASN_Org = take_any(DeviceCustomString4),
            TIMatch = take_any(ThreatDescription)
  by SourceIP
| where Attempts >= 5
| extend RuleSeverity = iff(isnotempty(TIMatch), "Critical", "High")
| order by Attempts desc

properties.description: Identifies 50+ firewall drop or reject events from the same source IP within an hour - likely a port scan, brute force probe, or misconfigured client. Uses UniFi zone-based firewall events parsed into CEF (DeviceEventClassID like LAN_LAN / WAN_LOCAL).
properties.displayName: UniFi: Repeated firewall drops from single source
properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor =~ "Ubiquiti"
| where DeviceEventCategory =~ "Firewall"
| where DeviceAction in~ ("DROP", "REJECT")
| where isnotempty(SourceIP)
| summarize Drops = count(),
            DistinctDstPorts = dcount(DestinationPort),
            Ports = make_set(DestinationPort, 50),
            ZoneDirs = make_set(DeviceEventClassID),
            Country = take_any(coalesce(MaliciousIPCountry, DeviceCustomString3)),
            ASN_Org = take_any(DeviceCustomString4),
            TIMatch = take_any(ThreatDescription)
  by SourceIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where Drops >= 50

properties.displayName: UniFi: Repeated firewall drops from single source

properties.description: Identifies successful SSH logins or admin CEF events originating from a source IP outside RFC1918 private ranges. These should be rare in a healthy network (VPN traffic should appear as private); investigate every match to confirm it was an authorised remote login.
properties.displayName: UniFi: Admin activity from non-LAN source
properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor =~ "Ubiquiti"
| where DeviceEventClassID == "sshd_auth_success"
        or DeviceEventCategory has_any ("admin", "auth", "login", "logon")
| where isnotempty(SourceIP)
| where not(ipv4_is_private(SourceIP))
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SourceIP, SourceUserName, DeviceAction,
          Activity, Message, DeviceEventClassID,
          Country = MaliciousIPCountry, ASN_Org = DeviceCustomString4,
          TIMatch = ThreatDescription, AdditionalExtensions

properties.displayName: UniFi: Admin activity from non-LAN source

properties.description: UnifiSyslogVpnBruteForce_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Identifies 10+ VPN authentication failures from a single source IP within one hour. Covers both CEF events in CommonSecurityLog and plain-syslog VPN failures from openvpn, wireguard, ipsec, strongswan, charon and xl2tpd daemons in Unifi_Syslog_CL.
properties.displayName: UniFi: VPN authentication failures (possible brute force)
properties.query:
let cef =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where DeviceVendor =~ "Ubiquiti"
    | where DeviceEventCategory has_any ("vpn", "auth")
    | where DeviceEventClassID != "sshd_auth_fail"
    | where DeviceAction has_any ("FAIL", "DENIED", "REJECT", "AUTH_FAIL")
            or Message has_any ("auth fail", "authentication failed", "incorrect password")
    | where isnotempty(SourceIP)
    | project TimeGenerated, SourceIP;
let plain =
    Unifi_Syslog_CL
    | where DeviceEventCategory =~ "vpn"
            or ProcessName has_any ("openvpn", "wireguard", "ipsec",
                                    "strongswan", "charon", "xl2tpd")
    | where DeviceAction has_any ("AUTH_FAIL", "FAIL", "DENIED")
            or Message has_any ("auth fail", "authentication failed",
                                "incorrect password")
    | where isnotempty(SourceIP)
    | project TimeGenerated, SourceIP;
union cef, plain
| summarize Failures = count() by SourceIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where Failures >= 10

properties.displayName: UniFi: VPN authentication failures (possible brute force)

properties.description: UnifiSyslogTorExitTraffic_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Identifies inbound traffic originating from a Tor exit node (identified at ingest time via the Tor exit-node feed). Legitimate use cases exist, but Tor is also a common cover for reconnaissance and credential stuffing.
properties.displayName: UniFi: Traffic from Tor exit node
properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where IndicatorThreatType has "Anonymization"
        or ThreatDescription has "tor_exit"
| summarize Hits = count(),
            TargetPorts = make_set(DestinationPort, 25),
            Activity = take_any(Activity),
            DeviceAction = take_any(DeviceAction),
            Country = take_any(MaliciousIPCountry)
  by SourceIP
| order by Hits desc

properties.displayName: UniFi: Traffic from Tor exit node

properties.description: Identifies critical-severity events emitted by UniFi devices - power loss, fan failure, hardware fault, or security crisis. Covers CEF events (LogSeverity 8+) in CommonSecurityLog and plain-syslog events with SyslogSeverity emergency/alert/critical.
properties.query:
let cef =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where DeviceVendor =~ "Ubiquiti"
    | where toint(LogSeverity) >= 8
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Severity = tostring(LogSeverity),
              ProcessName = DeviceProduct, Category = DeviceEventCategory,
              Message, Detail = AdditionalExtensions;
let plain =
    Unifi_Syslog_CL
    | where SyslogSeverity in~ ("emergency", "alert", "critical", "0", "1", "2")
            or DeviceEventCategory =~ "critical"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Severity = SyslogSeverity, ProcessName,
              Category = DeviceEventCategory, Message, Detail = tostring(RawJson);
union cef, plain

CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where isnotempty(MaliciousIP) and ThreatSeverity >= 5
| summarize Attempts = count(),
            Countries = make_set(MaliciousIPCountry, 5),
            Feeds = make_set(ThreatDescription, 5),
            ASN_Orgs = make_set(DeviceCustomString4, 5),
            TargetPorts = make_set(DestinationPort, 25),
            TargetUsers = make_set(SourceUserName, 25),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
  by SourceIP, ThreatSeverity, IndicatorThreatType
| order by Attempts desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Ranks TI-flagged source IPs by activity volume in the last 24h. Use this to prioritise blocklist additions or investigate persistent actors that hit your perimeter.

properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where DeviceEventCategory =~ "Firewall"
| where isnotempty(SourceIP) and not(ipv4_is_private(SourceIP))
| summarize DistinctPorts = dcount(DestinationPort),
            Ports = make_set(DestinationPort, 50),
            Attempts = count(),
            TIFlagged = take_any(ThreatDescription),
            Country = take_any(coalesce(MaliciousIPCountry, DeviceCustomString3)),
            ASN_Org = take_any(DeviceCustomString4)
  by SourceIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where DistinctPorts >= 10
| order by DistinctPorts desc, Attempts desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Lists external sources hitting 10 or more distinct destination ports within a 5-minute window - the classic port-scan signature. Filters to non-RFC1918 sources only to suppress noise from internal LAN scanners.

properties.displayName: UniFi: TI-flagged probes against application ports
properties.query:
let app_ports = dynamic([
    22, 23, 80, 443, 445, 1433, 3306, 3389, 5432, 5900, 8080, 8443, 32400, 4005
]);
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where isnotempty(MaliciousIP)
| where toint(DestinationPort) in (app_ports)
| summarize Attempts = count(),
            Sources = make_set(SourceIP, 25),
            Country = make_set(MaliciousIPCountry, 5),
            Feeds = make_set(ThreatDescription, 5),
            SampleSource = take_any(SourceIP)
  by DestinationPort
| order by Attempts desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Aggregates known-bad sources by application port being targeted (SSH, RDP, SMB, MSSQL, HTTP/S, Postgres, VNC, Plex, etc.). Reveals which of your services are getting the most scanner attention.

properties.displayName: UniFi: TI-flagged probes against application ports

properties.query:
let recent =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(1h) .. now())
    | where isnotempty(SourceIP) and not(ipv4_is_private(SourceIP))
    | distinct SourceIP;
let historic =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(1h))
    | where isnotempty(SourceIP)
    | distinct SourceIP;
recent
| where SourceIP !in (historic)
| join kind=leftouter (
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
    | summarize Activity = take_any(Activity),
                DeviceAction = take_any(DeviceAction),
                Hits = count(),
                Country = take_any(coalesce(MaliciousIPCountry, DeviceCustomString3)),
                ASN_Org = take_any(DeviceCustomString4),
                TIMatch = take_any(ThreatDescription)
      by SourceIP
  ) on SourceIP
| project SourceIP, Country, ASN_Org, Hits, Activity, DeviceAction, TIMatch
| order by Hits desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Lists external IPs observed in the last hour that have never appeared in the preceding 30 days. New-source anomalies often correlate with attack campaigns and reconnaissance waves.

properties.query:
let expected_countries = dynamic([ "GB", "US", "" ]);
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where DeviceEventClassID == "sshd_auth_success"
        or (DeviceEventCategory has_any ("admin", "auth") and DeviceVendor =~ "Ubiquiti")
| extend Country = coalesce(MaliciousIPCountry, DeviceCustomString3)
| where Country !in (expected_countries)
| project TimeGenerated, SourceIP, SourceUserName, Country,
          ASN_Org = DeviceCustomString4, Activity, DeviceAction,
          DeviceEventClassID, TIMatch = ThreatDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Lists admin or SSH activity originating from countries outside your normal operating geography. Edit the expected_countries list at the top of the query to match your environment.

properties.query:
let system_users = dynamic([ "unifi-protect", "unifi", "unifi-network",
                             "ubios-udapi", "ubios" ]);
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where DeviceEventClassID == "sudo_command"
| where SourceUserName !in (system_users)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SourceUserName, DestinationUserName,
          Command = Message, PWD = DeviceCustomString1,
          Product = DeviceProduct
| order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Lists sudo events not from known service accounts (unifi-protect, unifi, ubios-udapi). Real admin sudo activity to root warrants attention. Tune the system_users list at the top of the query for your environment.

properties.description: UnifiSyslogSshTargetUsers_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.0

properties.displayName: UniFi: SSH brute force - most-targeted accounts
properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where DeviceEventClassID == "sshd_auth_fail"
| summarize Attempts = count(),
            DistinctSources = dcount(SourceIP),
            TopCountries = make_set(coalesce(MaliciousIPCountry, DeviceCustomString3), 10),
            TIFlagged = countif(isnotempty(MaliciousIP))
  by TargetUser = SourceUserName
| order by Attempts desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Aggregates SSH authentication failures by the username being targeted. Reveals what attackers think your accounts are named - useful for tightening account lockdown and removing default usernames.

properties.displayName: UniFi: SSH brute force - most-targeted accounts

properties.description: UnifiSyslogWifiDeauthFlood_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.0

properties.displayName: UniFi: WiFi deauth flooding (possible Karma / Evil Twin)
properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where DeviceEventClassID in ("wifi_deauth", "wifi_disassoc")
| summarize Events = count(),
            DistinctClients = dcount(SourceMACAddress),
            Clients = make_set(SourceMACAddress, 25)
  by Computer, DeviceEventClassID, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Events >= 20
| order by Events desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Lists APs that observed 20 or more WiFi deauth or disassoc events within a 5-minute window. Could be normal roaming chaos, or active deauth attack (Karma, Evil Twin, WPA handshake capture).

properties.displayName: UniFi: WiFi deauth flooding (possible Karma / Evil Twin)

properties.description: UnifiSyslogDhcpNakDecline_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.0

properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where DeviceEventClassID in ("dhcp_NAK", "dhcp_DECLINE")
| summarize Events = count(),
            Clients = make_set(SourceMACAddress, 25),
            Hostnames = make_set(SourceHostName, 25),
            Interfaces = make_set(DeviceInboundInterface, 5),
            SampleClient = take_any(SourceMACAddress)
  by DeviceAction
| order by Events desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Lists DHCP NAK (server denied) and DECLINE (client refused offer, usually IP conflict) bursts. Volume spikes can mean rogue DHCP, address-pool exhaustion, or a misconfigured client.

properties.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(MaliciousIP)
| mv-expand Feed = split(ThreatDescription, ", ")
| summarize Hits = count(),
            UniqueSources = dcount(SourceIP),
            TopCountries = make_set(MaliciousIPCountry, 10),
            SampleSource = take_any(SourceIP)
  by Feed = tostring(Feed)
| order by Hits desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Lists how many hits each threat-intel feed produced over the last 7 days. Useful for deciding whether a feed is pulling its weight or needs replacing in the Logstash relay configuration.

metadata.description: The UniFi Syslog workbook delivers end-to-end forensic and security visibility over UDM / switch / AP syslog data. Eight tabs cover incidents, investigation, hunts, identity, world-map geo, firewall analysis, pipeline health, and a hero KPI overview - powered by the 8 analytic rules, 10 hunting queries, MaxMind GeoIP/ASN enrichment, and FireHOL/Spamhaus/Tor TI feeds shipped with the connector.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The UniFi Syslog solution for Microsoft Sentinel ingests UDM, switch and access-point syslog telemetry using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) Push pattern. A self-hosted Logstash relay parses the UniFi dual-timestamp format, normalises CEF and synthetic-CEF events, enriches with MaxMind GeoIP/ASN and FireHOL/Spamhaus/Tor threat-intel feeds at ingest time, and POSTs to the Data Collection Endpoint provisioned by this connector.

Pipeline split:

  • CEF + synthetic-CEF security events land in CommonSecurityLog (firewall zones, sudo, dnsmasq-dhcp, AP kernel WiFi, ubios gateway signals, switch link state, sshd).
  • Forensic / non-CEF events land in Unifi_Syslog_CL as a structured catch-all.

Ships: 8 analytic rules (TI match, IDS/IPS, SSH brute force, firewall drops, admin-from-WAN, VPN brute force, Tor traffic, critical-severity) and 10 hunting queries (top attackers, port scanners, app-port probes, first-seen sources, geo anomaly, sudo audit, SSH targets, WiFi deauth flood, DHCP anomalies, TI feed effectiveness).

Pre-requisites:

  1. A Linux host reachable from your UniFi devices on UDP/TCP 514 (UnRAID, Docker host, k8s, VM).
  2. The Logstash container shipped with this Solution under its Logstash/ subfolder (Dockerfile + logstash.conf + MaxMind/TI refreshers).
  3. (Optional but recommended) A MaxMind account for GeoIP/ASN enrichment - free GeoLite2 tier is sufficient.

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 8, Hunting Queries: 10
